text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English, the podcast where we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听《Real Easy English》，在这个播客里，我们用简单的英语进行真实的对话，帮助大家学习。

text: I'm Georgie. And I'm Neil. And you can watch a video version of this podcast
zh: 我是Georgie。我是Neil。你可以观看这个播客的视频版，

text: and get a free worksheet at bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 并在bbclearningenglish.com上获取免费的练习表。

text: Hi Neil. How are you? Hi Georgie. Yeah, I'm very good.
zh: 嗨，Neil，你好吗？嗨，Georgie，我很好。

text: I'm excited. Why? I'm going to a birthday party.
zh: 我很兴奋。为什么？因为我要去参加一个生日派对。

text: Whose birthday party? My sister's. Your sister!
zh: 谁的生日派对？我妹妹的。你妹妹！

text: Well, that's funny because today we are talking about brothers and sisters, or siblings.
zh: 这可真巧，因为我们今天正好要聊聊兄弟姐妹这个话题。

text: Yeah. Do you have any siblings, Georgie? I do. I have one sister.
zh: 是啊。Georgie，你有兄弟姐妹吗？有的，我有一个妹妹。

text: She is younger, but she is taller and more mature, and more sensible than I am.
zh: 她比我小，但她比我高，也比我成熟，比我更懂事。

text: How about you? Exactly the same. Yes, I have a younger sister, but she's a bit more grown up than me.
zh: 那你呢？完全一样。是的，我也有一个妹妹，但她比我更成熟一些。

text: A better version of you? Yeah, definitely.
zh: 是更好的一个版本的你？对，绝对是。

text: Are you similar? Well, are there any similarities between you and your
zh: 你们像吗？你和你妹妹之间有相似之处吗？

text: sister? Well, I mean, physically, we don't look similar, but some people say we do.
zh: 嗯，从外表上看我们不太像，但有人说我们像。

text: I can't really see it. it. But I think some aspects of our personality are similar.
zh: 我自己倒看不太出来，不过我觉得我们性格上有些方面是相似的。

text: We like some of the same things. It's, you know, it's good when we meet, but we don't meet very often.
zh: 我们喜欢一些相同的东西。见面的时候感觉挺好的，不过我们不常见面。

text: How often? Every few months. Yeah.
zh: 多久见一次？每隔几个月吧。是的。

text: Because, you know, she lives in a different city and has her family and life's busy.
zh: 因为她住在另一个城市，还要照顾家庭，生活很忙。

text: Yeah. I'm lucky because my sister lives in London.
zh: 是啊，我很幸运，我妹妹住在伦敦。

text: So we are able to see each other quite regularly. So, Georgie, are you close to your sister?
zh: 所以我们能经常见面。那Georgie，你和你妹妹关系亲近吗？

text: Yes, I would say that we are close.
zh: 是的，我会说我们关系很亲近。

text: Growing up we weren't so close, I would say, although I think she disagrees.
zh: 小时候我们其实不算亲近，虽然我觉得她可能不同意这个说法。

text: But since we went travelling together, we became closer
zh: 不过自从我们一起去旅行之后，我们就变得更亲近了，

text: because we had this kind of special experience together.
zh: 因为我们一起经历了那种特别的经历。

text: We were very far away from home. We had to do lots of difficult things, you know, organising.
zh: 我们远离家乡，要处理很多困难的事情，比如安排行程之类的。

text: We were, yeah, together against the world. So we became close. How about you? Are you close with your sister?
zh: 我们那时候就像是一起对抗全世界，所以关系变得亲近了。你呢？你和你妹妹亲近吗？

text: I think when we're together, we get on well, but then we can go a long period of time where we don't really talk.
zh: 我觉得我们在一起的时候相处得不错，但也常常会有很长一段时间不怎么联系。

text: And that's okay. It's life. So you have a sister and you get on well.
zh: 不过这也没关系，生活就是这样。所以你有一个妹妹，你们相处得不错。

text: But do you wish that you had more siblings? Yes, I wish I had a brother.
zh: 但你有没有希望自己能有更多兄弟姐妹？有，我希望我有个哥哥或弟弟。

text: It might sound strange, but I've always wanted to know what a boy in my family would look like.
zh: 这听起来可能有点奇怪，但我一直很想知道，如果我们家有个男孩会是什么样子。

text: Yeah, it might change the dynamic a little bit though. What about you?
zh: 是啊，不过那可能会让家庭氛围有点不一样。你呢？

text: Yeah, when I was younger, I thought it would be nice to have a brother, an older brother, to kind of show you the way.
zh: 嗯，我小时候觉得如果有个哥哥就好了，可以给你指引方向。

text: Because probably, you know, as the older sibling,
zh: 因为作为年长的兄弟姐妹，

text: you get to do everything first, and that's sometimes good and sometimes bad.
zh: 你会先经历所有事情，这有时候是好事，有时候是坏事。

text: But it's funny because the stereotype of older siblings is usually
zh: 不过有趣的是，人们对年长兄弟姐妹的刻板印象通常是，

text: that they are more more responsible and sensible because they had,
zh: 他们更负责任、更懂事，因为他们成长过程中规矩更多。

text: you know, more rules growing up. And then when the younger sibling comes, they have less rules or fewer rules.
zh: 而后来出生的弟弟妹妹，规矩就少一些。

text: But I think that my sister is the more serious and better behaved one.
zh: 但我觉得我妹妹反而是更严肃、更懂事的那一个。

text: Let's recap the vocabulary we heard in the conversation. We had siblings, which means brothers and sisters.
zh: 让我们来回顾一下对话中出现的词汇。我们听到了siblings，意思是兄弟姐妹。

text: We heard similar, which means almost but not exactly the
zh: 我们听到了similar，意思是几乎相同但不完全一样。

text: same. We had get on with, which means to have a good relationship with someone.
zh: 我们还听到了get on with，意思是与某人关系融洽。

text: For example, I get on well with my sister. We heard the adjective close.
zh: 比如说，我和我妹妹相处得很好。我们还听到了形容词close。

text: If two people are close, they like each other and they talk to each other often.
zh: 如果两个人close（亲近），意味着他们喜欢彼此，并且经常交流。

text: And that's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 好了，本期《Real Easy English》就到这里。

text: You can find a worksheet on our website and lots more episodes to help you practice.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站上找到练习表，还有更多的节目帮助你练习。

text: bbclearningenglish .com. See you then. Goodbye.
zh: bbclearningenglish.com，下次再见，拜拜。
